metadata.from: Bob Hanifen — return address reads 'Bob Hanifen' with military unit details at Sheppard Field, Texas
metadata.to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.location_sent_to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 704 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.envelope_notes: Return address reads: 'Bob Hanifen 17MO600 / Sqd. I-3706-A30 / AAF-BAS-B05 / Sheppard Fld Texas'. Pink stamp: 'POSTAGE DUE 2 CENTS'. A 2-cent postage due stamp (red, United States) is affixed on the right side. An airmail stamp is partially visible upper right, torn. Handwritten '103' near center. Letter addressed in cursive to 'Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 704 Keo Way, Des Moines Iowa'.
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting on return address is somewhat cramped; unit designations partially legible. Cursive address is flowing but readable. Postmark partially obscured.
metadata.historical_context: June 1945 — WWII still ongoing (V-E Day was May 8, 1945; war with Japan continuing). Bob is stationed at Sheppard Field, an AAF training base in Texas.

pages.transcription:
RETURN ADDRESS (upper left, printed/handwritten):
Bob Hanifen 17MO600
Sqd. I-3706-A30
AAF-BAS-B05
Sheppard Fld Texas

POSTMARK:
Wichita Falls [Texas], JUN 10, 2:30 PM, 1945

STAMP NOTATION (pink ink, center):
POSTAGE DUE 2 CENTS

ADDRESSED TO (center/right, cursive):
Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
704 Keo Way
Des Moines Iowa

OTHER:
Handwritten '103' near top center.
2-cent postage due stamp affixed lower right.
Airmail stamp (partially torn) upper right.

Wichita Falls [Texas], JUN 10, 2:30 PM, 1945

summary: This is the envelope for letter 31, sent by Bob Hanifen from Sheppard Field, Texas (an Army Air Forces base) to the Hanifen Body & Paint Co. at 704 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa, postmarked June 10, 1945. The envelope was assessed a 2-cent postage due charge, evidenced by both a pink rubber stamp and an affixed postage due stamp. No letter pages were provided — only the envelope image.
